body.title: [triage] Restore session-start skill-violation rollup in context-load (dropped in 10-step rewrite)
body.intention: I intend that context-load surfaces recent skill-rule violations at session start again, so rule-drift is visible on resume, with the feature, its spec entry, and its tests in agreement.
body.description:
FEATURE (follow-on to T-modernize-skill-writepath-tests-design-first; folds the disposition of T-context-load-violation-summary-missing). context-load/run originally surfaced a session-start skill-violation rollup (feat @3b0118e8) but the function violation_summary() was dropped during the 10-step orchestration rewrite @52f9dd42, and it is NOT in the current canonical spec (CLAUDE.md /context-load table + skills/context-load/SKILL.md both define exactly 10 steps with no violation step). During test-modernization the stale tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py (which called a non-existent violation_summary()) was DELETED rather than kept red, so the feature idea is captured here instead of lost.

Shape: add a violation-rollup step to skills/context-load/run that reads the skill-violation telemetry log (see D-skill-telemetry-2026-05-20 and T-skill-telemetry-logger for the log source/format), wire it into the run's main() briefing pipeline, add the step to CLAUDE.md's /context-load table + skills/context-load/SKILL.md so spec matches the executor, and re-author the 4 tests (empty-log-silent, absent-log-silent, shows-top-violations, respects-days-window).

**Scope boundary:** IN — restore the feature + spec entry + tests, agreeing with each other. OUT — changes to the skill-telemetry logger itself (its own ticket).
**Test plan:** proof-on-close — a test drives the restored rollup over a seeded telemetry log (top-N violations within the days window shown; empty/absent log stays silent), red before the feature exists, green after.
**Completion criteria:** context-load surfaces the rollup; CLAUDE.md table + SKILL.md list the step; the 4 behavior tests pass network-isolated.

Priority low (0.25): observability nice-to-have — Akien prioritizes. Related: T-context-load-violation-summary-missing (folded), T-skill-telemetry-logger, D-skill-telemetry-2026-05-20.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Note [2026-07-11 15:49]:** **Affected files:** skills/context-load/run, skills/context-load/SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md (/context-load table), tests/test_context_load_violation_summary.py (re-author).
